steps.name: Custom segmentation algorithm (only if @remotion/captions doesn't fit)
Wire segments into SceneRenderer props preparation
Wire up tests + property test + verify all Acceptance Criteria

decisions: @remotion/captions rejected: duration-window grouping has no hook for BR-1's number+unit cluster rule (see packages/remotion-templates/spike-captions-eval.md)
Custom segmentTimestamps.ts algorithm covers BR-1/BR-2/BR-4 with a two-pass greedy-group + short-segment-merge approach; property-tested for no text loss

SceneRenderer.tsx's SceneProps type hand-extended with voice/subtitle fields matching backend/app/schemas/scene.py field names exactly, since no generated TS type exists yet for the full Scene model (schema.ts only emits a runtime zod validator) — documented in prepareSubtitleProps.ts as a known gap, not fixed here (out of scope for 2-5)

Discovered packages/remotion-templates node_modules was effectively empty in this worktree (remotion, zod-from-json-schema unresolved) — ran npm install to unblock the full test suite; this was a pre-existing environment gap, not introduced by 2-5, and fixed 3 previously-failing test files (scene-renderer.test.ts, schema.fixtures.test.ts) as a side effect
